= GitHub Actions Workflows
:description: CI/CD automation for paint-type

This directory contains GitHub Actions workflow files that automate various aspects
of continuous integration, testing, security, and compliance for the paint-type repository.

== Purpose

The workflows in this directory:

* Automatically test code changes on pull requests and pushes
* Enforce project quality standards and compliance requirements
* Provide feedback to contributors on code health
* Ensure the repository remains in a deployable state
* Protect against security vulnerabilities and supply-chain attacks

== Contents by Functional Grouping

=== Core CI/CD

* `rust.yml`:: Rust language workflow. Runs `cargo check`, `cargo clippy`, `cargo fmt`,
  `cargo test`, and `cargo doc` for the paint_core Rust crate. Triggered on pushes and
  pull requests to main and release branches.

* `idris-ci.yml`:: Idris2 language workflow. Compiles Idris2 source files and runs
  Idris2-specific tests. Ensures ABI and FFI implementations are correct.

* `e2e.yml`:: End-to-end testing workflow. Runs the complete integration test suite
  across multiple components. Validates that the entire system works together.

=== Security and Compliance

* `scorecard-enforcer.yml`:: Runs OpenSSF Scorecard checks to ensure the repository
  meets security best practices. Checks for signed releases, branch protection,
  dependency updates, and other security-related metrics.

* `openssf-compliance.yml`:: Comprehensive OpenSSF compliance validation. Ensures
  adherence to security standards and best practices.

* `guix-nix-policy.yml`:: Enforces Guix and Nix package management policies. Blocks
  usage of non-approved package managers and ensures reproducible builds.

=== Language and Tool Policy

* `npm-bun-blocker.yml`:: Blocks any usage of npm or bun package managers. Enforces
  Deno as the approved JavaScript runtime.

* `ts-blocker.yml`:: Blocks TypeScript file creation or modification. Enforces
  ReScript or other approved alternatives.

=== Quality and Maintenance

* `coverage.yml`:: Measures and reports code coverage. Uses `cargo-llvm-cov` for
  Rust and best-effort coverage for other languages. Reports are informational
  and do not block merges.

* `workflow-linter.yml`:: Lints all GitHub Actions workflow files to ensure they
  follow best practices, have proper metadata, and are syntactically correct.

* `dogfood-gate.yml`:: Validates that the project uses its own tools and libraries
  (dogfooding). Ensures internal consistency and quality.

* `host.yml`:: Host-specific configuration and validation workflow.

* `mirror.yml`:: Repository mirroring and synchronization workflow.

== Purpose of Each Workflow

=== rust.yml

Primary CI workflow for Rust code. Ensures:

* Code compiles without errors
* Clippy linting passes with no warnings
* Code formatting follows project standards
* All unit and integration tests pass
* Documentation builds successfully

This is the gatekeeper for any Rust code changes in paint-type.

=== idris-ci.yml

Idris2-specific validation. Ensures:

* All Idris2 files compile successfully
* ABI definitions are correct
* FFI interfaces are properly defined
* Idris2-specific proofs and validations pass

Critical for maintaining the cross-language bridge between Idris2 and Zig/Rust.

=== e2e.yml

End-to-end integration testing. Validates:

* Component interoperability
* ABI/FFI correctness across languages
* Complete workflow from input to output
* System-level invariants and guarantees

Runs the most comprehensive test suite available.

=== scorecard-enforcer.yml

Security best practice validation. Checks:

* Branch protection settings
* Signed releases
* Dependency update frequency
* Security policy presence
* Code review practices
* Issue resolution timeliness

Helps maintain a high security posture for the repository.

== Workflow Triggers

Most workflows are triggered by:

* `push` to main branch
* `pull_request` to main branch
* `schedule` (nightly or weekly)
* `workflow_dispatch` (manual trigger)

Check individual workflow files for specific trigger configurations.

== SHA Pinning

All GitHub Actions used in these workflows should be SHA-pinned to specific commits
to prevent supply-chain attacks from compromised or updated actions.

Example:
[source,yaml]
----
- uses: actions/checkout@8e5e7e5ab8b370d3a0e0e70878d379440678a716 # v3.3.0
----
